frame_analyses.description: Setup view showing Penrose drain with visible slit and suture material ready for loading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear view of marked drain with slit, needle and instruments positioned

frame_analyses.description: Both needle drivers positioning to grasp needle, right hand driver approaching needle
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate instrument positioning, preparing for needle grasp

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle, preparing for first suture placement through drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp position, steady approach to target

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through drain material, suture being pulled through
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle passage, appropriate suture length being drawn through

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot formation, right hand wrapping suture around left instrument
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Starting surgeon's knot with double throw technique

frame_analyses.description: Completing first throw with double wrap, pulling suture through loop
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good technique for surgeon's knot formation, appropriate tension

frame_analyses.description: Left hand leading second throw, wrapping around right instrument
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching, left hand now leading as required

frame_analyses.description: Right hand leading third throw, final knot completion
Scissors cutting suture ends close to knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate suture length being left, clean cutting technique

penalties.description: Entry point appears approximately 1-2mm from optimal mark placement
Exit point appears approximately 1mm from optimal mark placement

knot_assessments.notes: Proper double throw technique for surgeon's knot
Good hand switching and single throw technique
Final throw completed with proper hand alternation

confidence_rationale: Clear visualization of most phases, but suture placement accuracy assessment limited by camera angle. Knot sequence and hand switching clearly observed. Time measurement based on visible task progression.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter measurements of suture deviation due to camera perspective
Precise needle entry angle assessment from overhead view
technique_summary: Solid intermediate-level performance with proper knot sequence and hand alternation. Time falls in intermediate range with some efficiency loss during first throw formation.
strengths: Consistent hand switching between all throws as required
improvement_suggestions: Reduce time on first throw formation from 90s to under 60s
Focus on more precise needle placement to minimize deviation from marks
Practice faster needle loading to reduce overall completion time
